change_comments.message: Will get others to review as well and then you can incorporate the comments in one go.

Reviewed the code with Team members and these are the aggregated comments we came up with.
Let us know if you agree and can incorporate the same if you do.
Reach out for any doubts.
fine with the changes now as most of the review comments are incorporated now.
approved with minor comments (on testware only), which can be handled with release notes

inline_comments.message:
do u think we can make the formal args of the below 3 methods similar.
getAddedDeltaReports
getRemovedDeltaReports
getUpdatedDeltaReports

for consistency. Eventually it might remove some of the duplicacy if we have it.
I have tried to make the arguments consistent, you can have a look and tell me if you agree with that approach
NB: i think the impl. in patch#2 is more appropriate where u have separate methods for removed and updated delta report.

NB: same here as above.
can be a small separate method.

yes, I wanted to discuss about it, but I can put it up here.
So basically this test just checks if there are expected number of changes(deltas) in a delta report or not, nothing else. So i  think we can remove it as it is achievable by other tests as well.
this check is not required. plz check.

NB: can be broken into multiple statements.

eg: 
then : the delta report contains "update" action
and : (then you can validate the data separately).

NB: leaf modified in target anchor maybe ??
or something short ( for quick readability )
NB: leaf removed in target anchor
NB: split into multiple blocks/statements for readability and logical grouping.

NB: can also be directly used instead of assigning to variable.
The methods here are also self-explanatory and also its not a long call/signature.
So readability is also not impacted.
yes it can be removed. Thanks
Marking this as resolved as this test was not bringing any value. I have removed it
changed to "leaf is modified in target anchor"

i removed the parent node xpath, just to be sure that only child node xpaths are in "xpathsInDeltaReport", but works without having that extra step as well.
In general this part is checking that xpaths and data of child nodes are present in delta report.
Fixed it and removed the extra statement

directly use in the method.
i.e inline it.

extract to method.
maybe hasAnyUpdates boolean method??

NB: use static.
same applies to other variables as well below.
NB: can also assert that the target data is not present.

where clause can be removed.
Only test it with leaf data scenario. (sourceDataNodeWithLeafData)
can remove the other.
NB: can also assert that the source data is not present.

where clause can be removed.
Only test it with leaf data scenario. (targetDataNodeWithLeafData)
can remove the other.
Leaves Delta Report for parent and child
NB: instead of dataNode1 and dataNode2 call it sourceDataNode and targetDataNode respectively.

build the datanode array using the groovy constructor.
[new DataNode( xpath: '/parent' , leaves: ['parent-leaf': 'parent-payload']) , new DataNode(...)]

same comment applies for other places as well.

where clause not required.
create 2 AND blocks to assert the parent and then child.
result.first() can be result[0] wherver applicable.
can add one more scenario where we can handle multiple leaves as well.
can get rid off the entire 'where' clause and do multiple assertions.

multiple separations are not required here.
use |
since the xpath is always the same so inline it.

if these values are always the same then u can hardocde in the test.
No need to send as parameters.
static annotation? or did you mean static variables?
just out of curiosity...why should I avoid testing "no leaf data" scenario?
sorry I did not understand this comment. Can you explain?
it checks if parent and child are Updated individually or not, as both get reported separately in the delta report so I think where clause is needed for testing them separately.

again, I did not fully understand this. Do you want me to change the whole Heading or just a part of it
I have changed it to "Delta report between leaves #scenario" in the meantime

did you mean like this?
    def 'Get delta between anchors for when #scenario'() {
        when: 'attempt to get delta report between anchors'
            def result = objectUnderTest.getDeltaByDataspaceAndAnchors(FUNCTIONAL_TEST_DATASPACE_3, BOOKSTORE_ANCHOR_3, BOOKSTORE_ANCHOR_5, '/', OMIT_DESCENDANTS)
        then: 'delta report contains expected number of changes'
            result.size() == 3
        and: 'delta report contains UPDATE action with expected xpath'
            assert result[0].getAction() == 'update'
            assert result[0].getXpath() == "/bookstore"
        and: 'delta report contains REMOVE action with expected xpath'
            assert result[1].getAction() == 'remove'
            assert result[1].getXpath() == "/bookstore-address[@bookstore-name='Easons-1']"
        and: 'delta report contains ADD action with expected xpath'
            assert result[2].getAction() == 'add'
            assert result[2].getXpath() == "/bookstore-address[@bookstore-name='Crossword Bookstores']"
    }

applies to the slogan.
can change it to the above.

i think this is also fine.
Delta report between leaves #scenario"
in groovy , try to use single-quotes everywhere instead of double-quotes

yes.. something like this.
without the where clause and explicitly adding the assertions.
sry I meant for all @Shared annotation.. you can use static.
ok, changed to "Delta Report between leaves for parent and child nodes #scenario".

This method will NOT be called when both leave are nul. Therefor you do NOT need the condition on line #170. 
AS is clear from the coverage report that condition is not covered for false.
You can simplify the code as shown in my next comment

it is not really a different scenario. It doesnt help describing the behavior any betetr. SO it is only extra maintenance
Over time redundant test that dont add value can really become a hindrance when they need to be updated...
NB: no scenarios anymore. Is the first part of the title enough for this test (I think so)
NB: no scenarios anymore. Is the first part of the title enough for this test (I think so)

NB: you are NOT executing 2 test. but test the same thing twice but asserting first the parent and then the child.

Also no need to describe each assert especially if if the assert code is very clear. Instead focus your descriptions on the significant part/word in this case parent and child. SO no need for where but re-write your asserts like this:

and: 'the delta report contain the correct details for the parent'
  assert ...
  assert ...
  assert ...  
  
and: 'the delta report contain the correct details for the child'
  assert ...
  assert ...
  assert ...
suggestion: I would just call this 'otherBookstore'

I kept the name specific to Delta because if anything is changed in the file it will lead to multiple test failures, So, if any developer is working on this they should know exactly what this json will impact upon.
